24.text: Yes, truly; for no sin is greater or more provoking to God than the profaning of his name; and therefore he has commanded it to be punished with death.

22.text: That I show all honour, love, and fidelity to my father and mother, and to all in authority over me; that I submit myself with due obedience to all their good instruction and correction; and also that

33.text: We believe that the same good God, after He had created all things, did not forsake them or give them up to fortune or chance, but that He rules and governs them according to His holy will, so that no

17.text: Marriage is to be between one man and one woman: neither is it lawful for any man to have more than one wife, nor for any woman to have more than one husband, at the same time.
The eighth commandment forbiddeth whatsoever doth or may unjustly hinder our own or our neighbour's wealth or outward estate.

5.text: The ninth commandment requireth the maintaining and promoting of truth between man and man, and of our own and our neighbour's good name, especially in witness-bearing.

34.text: That the word may become effectual to salvation, we must attend thereunto with diligence, preparation, and prayer; receive it with faith and love, lay it up in our hearts, and practise it in our lives

8.text: In the fourth petition, which is Give us this day our daily bread, we pray that of God's free gift we may receive a competent portion of the good things of this life, and enjoy his blessing with them.

3.text:
God's works of providence are his most holy, wise, and powerful preserving and governing all his creatures; ordering them, and all their actions, to his own glory.
            ( Ps. 145:17 ; Heb. 1:3
